title: Closed-loop geothermal starts with hitting the target.
intro: Next-generation geothermal depends on intentionally connecting wellbores thousands of feet underground — the hardest part of the well design. Gunnar brings the magnetic ranging discipline that made relief wells and SAGD possible to the geothermal drilling engineer's toolbox.

heroStats.l: precise spacing to access heat reservoirs

challengeTitle: Heat isn't the bottleneck. Hitting the target is.
challenge: Closed-loop and enhanced geothermal systems require two wells to intersect precisely — or multiple wellbores to be spaced exactly to access specific zones of a heat reservoir. At depth, conventional surveys simply aren't accurate enough to guarantee a connection.
Without precise placement, a closed-loop design fails and the economics of the project collapse.
approachTitle: The proven physics of well intersection.
approach: Magnetic ranging deterministically guides one wellbore to intersect another — the same technique that made relief well drilling, SAGD and coal-bed-methane intersect drilling possible in oil & gas.
Gunnar deploys active and passive ranging to form closed loops and to space multiple wellbores accurately across a reservoir, unlocking geothermal designs that were previously impossible.
capTitle: Precision placement for the geothermal era.

capabilities.b: Intentionally connect two wellbores to form a deep closed-loop system.
Precise placement of multiple wells to access specific heat zones.
Parallel placement at controlled separation for paired-well designs.
Intercept geometry and trajectory design for connection confidence.
Real-time steering integrated with the ranging solution.
Proximity and collision management across the well field.

tech.name: Torped™
tech.note: Downhole rotary magnet generates a strong active field — ideal for intersection and twinning at range.

tech.name: DeadSet™
tech.note: No-access active ranging for precise well-to-well connection with ±1 ft accuracy.

tech.name: EverReady™
tech.note: AI-driven passive ranging for spacing and proximity control using existing survey data.

caseLoc: Featured by ThinkGeoEnergy & Geothermal Resources Council
caseBody: Gunnar's President detailed how magnetic ranging — the technology behind relief wells and SAGD — is now enabling closed-loop geothermal by purposely intersecting two wells and precisely spacing wellbores to access particular zones of a heat reservoir, unlocking designs not previously available to industry.
caseMetricL: ranging toolset for the geothermal drilling engineer

faqs.q: Why is magnetic ranging essential for geothermal?
faqs.a: Closed-loop geothermal requires two wellbores to intersect precisely at depth, and multi-zone designs require exact spacing. Conventional surveys aren't accurate enough to guarantee connection; magnetic ranging provides the deterministic precision needed.

faqs.q: What is a closed-loop geothermal system?
faqs.a: It's a deep, sealed loop — like a heat pump far underground — formed by drilling two wells that intersect. Fluid circulates through the loop to extract heat without producing reservoir fluids. Ranging makes the connection possible.

faqs.q: Can ranging space multiple wells accurately?
faqs.a: Yes. The same technology used to intersect wells is used to hold precise separation between wellbores, allowing operators to target specific zones of a heat reservoir.
Yes. Magnetic ranging made relief well, SAGD and coal-bed-methane intersect drilling possible in oil & gas. Gunnar is applying that same proven physics to geothermal.
